message:
fix(validation): allow @ character in profile names for email-based naming

The README extensively documents using email addresses as profile names
(e.g., alice@gmail.com, work@company.com), but the validation functions
were rejecting the @ character. This was overly restrictive since:

1. The @ character has no special meaning in bash (not a shell metacharacter)
2. Email addresses are natural identifiers for accounts
3. Many tests and docs use email-style profile names

Updated validateVaultSegment and validateStoreSegment to allow @.
Updated tests to include email-based profile names in valid list.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -193,8 +193,7 @@ func TestVaultBackup(t *testing.T) {
 			`profile"doublequoted"`,
 			// Spaces (word splitting)
 			"profile with spaces",
-			// Other special characters
-			"profile@email",
+			// Other special characters (@ is allowed for email-based names)
 			"profile#hashtag",
 			"profile$var",
 			"profile%mod",
@@ -222,6 +221,9 @@ func TestVaultBackup(t *testing.T) {
 			"with.period",
 			"MixedCase123",
 			"profile-1.backup_v2",
+			"alice@gmail.com",
+			"work@company.com",
+			"profile@email",
 		}
 
 		for _, name := range validNames {
